codeReview.summary: The feature largely implements the requested path/branding rename and adds strong verification tests, but it leaves a blocking runtime inconsistency in smoke onboarding flows: the Docker image still invokes the old package name and the version variable rename is only partially propagated across workflow/script/Dockerfile boundaries.

issues.description: The onboarding smoke container still runs `npx --yes "paperclipai@${PAPIERKLAMMER_VERSION}" ...` instead of the renamed package. This contradicts the CLI rename intent and can fail once only `papierklammer` is published.

The script still passes `--build-arg PAPERCLIPAI_VERSION=...` and uses `PAPERCLIPAI_VERSION` (e.g., line 7), while `Dockerfile.onboard-smoke` now expects `PAPIERKLAMMER_VERSION` and `release-smoke.yml` sets `PAPIERKLAMMER_VERSION` (line 69). The requested smoke version is no longer wired through correctly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rename-worker skill procedure hardcodes `rg` for verification, but this environment does not provide `rg`; workers fall back to other search mechanisms.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/rename-worker/SKILL.md step 3 requires ripgrep; transcript/handoff for session c1f0b8f2-5b35-4412-8a21-b277a2030875 shows grep-based verification commands.

summary: Reviewed handoff, commit diff, skill, and transcript skeleton. The rename is mostly complete, but the smoke Docker path has blocking old/new CLI naming and version-variable wiring defects, so this feature should not pass as-is.
